title: Benchmark raw event (Contract 3)
description: One record per request, emitted by inferbench as JSONL (one JSON object per line). Normative. Raw events are the pooling unit for benchmark-result percentiles: results MUST be computed from these records, never from per-run summaries. All latencies are client-side measurements in seconds (the client-side mirror series of metrics/metrics.md; the gateway-side series are separate by definition). Latency measurement basis (v0.2.0, coordinated-omission safety): client-side TTFT and end-to-end latency are measured from scheduled_send_ts, never from send_ts. Nullable required fields assert 'not applicable', never 'not recorded'.

run_id.description: Manifest run_id this event belongs to.

repetition.description: 1-based repetition index within the run set (manifest.repetitions).

request_id.description: The X-Request-Id of the request (Contract 1); joins this record to gateway traces and usage records.

workload_item.description: Deterministic index or id of the workload item that generated this request (reproducible from workload version + seed).

scheduled_send_ts.description: Schedule-plan send time for this request: when the workload's arrival process dictated it be sent, independent of whether the client could actually send it then. NORMATIVE measurement rule (coordinated-omission safety): client-side TTFT and end-to-end latency are measured from scheduled_send_ts, NOT send_ts, so that dispatch/connect/write delay (client-side queueing under saturation) is never excluded from the latency a request experienced; send_ts remains the actual wire-write time for diagnostic comparison.

send_ts.description: Client timestamp when the request body write completed (actual wire-write time). Diagnostic: NOT the latency measurement basis — that is scheduled_send_ts. send_ts - scheduled_send_ts is the send slip (see send_slip_seconds).

send_slip_seconds.description: OPTIONAL observability field: send_ts - scheduled_send_ts, the delay between the scheduled and actual send. Sustained growth is the signature of client-side saturation (the generator can no longer keep up with its own schedule) and belongs in the result's validity block when material.

end_ts.description: Client timestamp when the response stream closed (success, error, cancellation, or shed).

status.description: Terminal outcome as observed by the client. shed = typed 429/503 rejection before any output.

error_class.description: Contract 1 error taxonomy value; null exactly when status is ok.

ttft_seconds.description: Client-side TTFT: scheduled_send_ts to first response body byte at the client (NOT send_ts — dispatch/connect/write queueing counts against latency; coordinated-omission safety). Null when no body byte ever arrived (shed, pre-first-token error).

itl.description: Inter-token/inter-chunk latency record: either the full gap series or a summary, always with the maximum stall. Null for requests with fewer than two content chunks. Pooled ITL percentiles in benchmark-result require the series form; summary-only events cannot be pooled and that limitation MUST appear in the result's validity block.

series_seconds.description: Gaps between consecutive content-bearing chunks, in arrival order.

max_stall_seconds.description: Largest single inter-chunk gap observed in this stream (the stall signal; compared against the SLO stall threshold for stall_rate).

input_tokens.description: Input token count per the manifest tokenizer (from the usage payload when available).

output_tokens.description: Output tokens received before termination. Tokens emitted before cancellation are billable (Contract 1) and count here.

shed.description: True when the request was rejected by admission control (429/503 with Retry-After).

retries.description: Number of retries performed for this logical request. Retries are pre-first-token only (Contract 1: mid-stream failures are never retried).

cancellation_point.description: Where the client canceled; null exactly when the request was not canceled.

elapsed_seconds.description: Seconds after send_ts at which the client closed the connection.

output_tokens_at_cancel.description: Output tokens received when the cancel was issued.
